steps.title: Identify the contextual signal
steps.content: McGrattan's papers being "frequently cited" by other scholars, who "clearly find her studies valuable," both point to the same direction: her work is useful. The blank should connect frequent citation to highlighting usefulness.

steps.title: Match the precise word
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean emphasizes or highlights — frequent citation is presented as evidence of usefulness, so the blank connects those two ideas positively. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — forestalls: means prevents or holds off — implies frequent citation would block acknowledgment of usefulness, which contradicts the passage's logic. ✗
  • Choice B — belies: means contradicts or gives a false impression of — implies the citation frequency contradicts her usefulness, the opposite of what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice C — underscores: means emphasizes or draws attention to — frequent citation is direct evidence that highlights her research's usefulness to peers. ✓
  • Choice D — overshadows: means eclipses or diminishes by comparison — implies citation frequency actually obscures her usefulness, which contradicts the supportive tone. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
